testcase.name: test_toy_package

failure.message: AssertionError: assert 1 == 0 + where 1 = <Result FileNotFoundError(2, 'No such file or directory')>.exit_code

testcase.failure:
@pytest.mark.skipif(sys.platform == "win32", reason="does not run on windows") @pytest.mark.integration def test_toy_package(): """Test using edgetest with a toy package.""" runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("setup.cfg", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_CFG) with open("setup.py", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_PY) # Make a directory for the module Path(loc, "toy_package").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "toy_package", "__init__.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(MODULE_CODE) # Make a directory for the tests Path(loc, "tests").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "tests", "test_main.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(TEST_CODE)
# Run the CLI result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=setup.cfg"])
> assert result.exit_code == 0 E AssertionError: assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result FileNotFoundError(2, 'No such file or directory')>.exit_code
/Users/nelsonmbigili/Desktop/Spring 2026/Software Analysis/migratelib-artifact/repos/capitalone@edgetest__dfc8b73c__tabulate__prettytable/tests/test_integration_cfg.py:145: AssertionError

@pytest.mark.skipif(sys.platform == "win32", reason="does not run on windows") @pytest.mark.integration def test_toy_package_lower(): """Test lower bounds with a toy package.""" runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("setup.cfg", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_CFG_LOWER) with open("setup.py", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_PY) # Make a directory for the module Path(loc, "toy_package").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "toy_package", "__init__.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(MODULE_CODE) # Make a directory for the tests Path(loc, "tests").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "tests", "test_main.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(TEST_CODE)

@pytest.mark.integration def test_toy_package_extras(): """Test using edgetest with a toy package.""" runner = CliRunner()

@pytest.mark.skipif(sys.platform == "win32", reason="does not run on windows") @pytest.mark.integration def test_toy_package(): """Test using edgetest with a toy package.""" runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("pyproject.toml", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_TOML) with open("setup.py", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_PY) # Make a directory for the module Path(loc, "toy_package").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "toy_package", "__init__.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(MODULE_CODE) # Make a directory for the tests Path(loc, "tests").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "tests", "test_main.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(TEST_CODE)
# Run the CLI result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=pyproject.toml"])
> assert result.exit_code == 0 E AssertionError: assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result FileNotFoundError(2, 'No such file or directory')>.exit_code

@pytest.mark.skipif(sys.platform == "win32", reason="does not run on windows") @pytest.mark.integration def test_toy_package_lower(): """Test using edgetest with a toy package.""" runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("pyproject.toml", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_TOML_LOWER) with open("setup.py", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_PY) # Make a directory for the module Path(loc, "toy_package").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "toy_package", "__init__.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(MODULE_CODE) # Make a directory for the tests Path(loc, "tests").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "tests", "test_main.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(TEST_CODE)
# Run the CLI result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=pyproject.toml"])
> assert result.exit_code == 0 E AssertionError: assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result FileNotFoundError(2, 'No such file or directory')>.exit_code

@pytest.mark.integration def test_toy_package_extras(): """Test using edgetest with a toy package.""" runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("pyproject.toml", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_TOML_EXTRAS) with open("setup.py", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_PY) # Make a directory for the module Path(loc, "toy_package").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "toy_package", "__init__.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(MODULE_CODE) # Make a directory for the tests Path(loc, "tests").mkdir() with open(Path(loc, "tests", "test_main.py"), "w") as outfile: outfile.write(TEST_CODE)
# Run the CLI result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=pyproject.toml", "--export"])
> assert result.exit_code == 0 E AssertionError: assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result FileNotFoundError(2, 'No such file or directory')>.exit_code

testcase.name: test_cli_basic

failure.message: AssertionError: expected call not found. Expected: create(PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp00oe30qp/.edgetest/myenv')) Actual: create(env_dir=PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp00oe30qp/.edgetest/myenv'))

testcase.failure:
mock_popen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4567288784'> mock_cpopen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4570052112'> mock_builder = <MagicMock name='EnvBuilder' spec='EnvBuilder' id='4569642192'>
@patch("edgetest.lib.EnvBuilder", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.core.Popen", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_basic(mock_popen, mock_cpopen, mock_builder): """Test creating a basic environment.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0) mock_cpopen.return_value.communicate.return_value = ("output", "error") type(mock_cpopen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("setup.cfg", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_CFG)
result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=setup.cfg"])
assert result.exit_code == 0
env_loc = Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myenv" if platform.system() == "Windows": py_loc = env_loc / "Scripts" / "python.exe" else: py_loc = env_loc / "bin" / "python"
> mock_builder.return_value.create.assert_called_with(env_loc)
/Users/nelsonmbigili/Desktop/Spring 2026/Software Analysis/migratelib-artifact/repos/capitalone@edgetest__dfc8b73c__tabulate__prettytable/tests/test_interface_cfg.py:148: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='EnvBuilder().create' spec='function' id='4569701584'> args = (PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp00oe30qp/.edgetest/myenv'),) kwargs = {} expected = call('', (PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp00oe30qp/.edgetest/myenv'),), {}) actual = call('', (PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp00oe30qp/.edgetest/myenv'),), {}) _error_message = <function NonCallableMock.assert_called_with.<locals>._error_message at 0x110758fe0> cause = None
def assert_called_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the last call was made with the specified arguments.
Raises an AssertionError if the args and keyword args passed in are different to the last call to the mock.""" if self.call_args is None: expected = self._format_mock_call_signature(args, kwargs) actual = 'not called.' error_message = ('expected call not found.\nExpected: %s\nActual: %s' % (expected, actual)) raise AssertionError(error_message)
def _error_message(): msg = self._format_mock_failure_message(args, kwargs) return msg expected = self._call_matcher(_Call((args, kwargs), two=True)) actual = self._call_matcher(self.call_args) if actual != expected: cause = expected if isinstance(expected, Exception) else None > raise AssertionError(_error_message()) from cause E AssertionError: expected call not found. E Expected: create(PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp00oe30qp/.edgetest/myenv')) E Actual: create(env_dir=PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp00oe30qp/.edgetest/myenv'))
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:939: AssertionError

@patch("edgetest.lib.EnvBuilder", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.core.Popen", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_basic_lower(mock_popen, mock_cpopen, mock_builder): """Test creating a basic environment.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0) mock_cpopen.return_value.communicate.return_value = ("output", "error") type(mock_cpopen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("setup.cfg", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_CFG_LOWER)
result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=setup.cfg"])
assert result.exit_code == 0
env_loc = Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myenv_lower" if platform.system() == "Windows": py_loc = env_loc / "Scripts" / "python.exe" else: py_loc = env_loc / "bin" / "python"

def assert_called_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the last call was made with the specified arguments.
Raises an AssertionError if the args and keyword args passed in are different to the last call to the mock.""" if self.call_args is None: expected = self._format_mock_call_signature(args, kwargs) actual = 'not called.' error_message = ('expected call not found.\nExpected: %s\nActual: %s' % (expected, actual)) raise AssertionError(error_message)

testcase.failure:
mock_popen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4571721168'> mock_cpopen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4572128336'> mock_builder = <MagicMock name='EnvBuilder' spec='EnvBuilder' id='4572578064'>
@patch("edgetest.lib.EnvBuilder", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.core.Popen", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_reqs(mock_popen, mock_cpopen, mock_builder): """Test running tests based on the requirements file.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0) mock_cpopen.return_value.communicate.return_value = ("output", "error") type(mock_cpopen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("requirements.txt", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(REQS)
result = runner.invoke(cli)
if platform.system() == "Windows": py_myupgrade_loc = ( Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myupgrade" / "Scripts" / "python.exe" ) py_allreq_loc = ( Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "all-requirements" / "Scripts" / "python.exe" ) else: py_myupgrade_loc = Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myupgrade" / "bin" / "python" py_allreq_loc = Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "all-requirements" / "bin" / "python"
assert result.exit_code == 0
> assert mock_builder.return_value.create.call_args_list == [ call(env_dir=Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myupgrade"), call(env_dir=Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "all-requirements"), ] E AssertionError: assert [call(env_dir...quirements'))] == [call(env_dir...quirements'))] E E At index 0 diff: call(env_dir=PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp0rfzyq6p/.edgetest/myupgrade')) != call(env_dir=PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp0rfzyq6p/.edgetest/myupgrade')) E E Full diff: E - [ E - call(env_dir=PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp0rfzyq6p/.edgetest/myupgrade')), E ? ^^^^... E E ...Full output truncated (7 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show

@patch("edgetest.core.Popen", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_nosetup(mock_popen, mock_cpopen): """Test creating a basic environment.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0) mock_cpopen.return_value.communicate.return_value = ("output", "error") type(mock_cpopen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("setup.cfg", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_CFG)
result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=setup.cfg", "--nosetup"])
assert result.exit_code == 0
env_loc = str(Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myenv") if platform.system() == "Windows": py_loc = Path(env_loc) / "Scripts" / "python.exe" else: py_loc = Path(env_loc) / "bin" / "python"
> assert mock_popen.call_args_list == [ call( ("uv", "pip", "list", f"--python={py_loc}", "--format", "json"), stdout=-1, stderr=-1, universal_newlines=True, ), ] E AssertionError: assert [call(('uv', ...ewlines=True)] == [call(('uv', ...ewlines=True)] E E At index 0 diff: call(('uv', 'pip', 'list', '--python=/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmpjct4bdw0/.edgetest/myenv/bin/python', '--format', 'json'), stdout=-1, stderr=-1, universal_newlines=True) != call(('uv', 'pip', 'list', '--python=/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmpjct4bdw0/.edgetest/myenv/bin/python', '--format', 'json'), stdout=-1, stderr=-1, universal_newlines=True) E E Full diff: E - [ E - call(('uv', 'pip', 'list', '--python=/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmpjct4bdw0/.edgetest/myenv/bin/python', '--format', '... mock_popen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4571182032'> mock_cpopen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4572149520'>
@patch("edgetest.core.Popen", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_nosetup_lower(mock_popen, mock_cpopen): """Test creating a basic environment.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0) mock_cpopen.return_value.communicate.return_value = ("output", "error") type(mock_cpopen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("setup.cfg", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_CFG_LOWER)
result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=setup.cfg", "--nosetup"])
assert result.exit_code == 0
env_loc = str(Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myenv_lower") if platform.system() == "Windows": py_loc = Path(env_loc) / "Scripts" / "python.exe" else: py_loc = Path(env_loc) / "bin" / "python"

@patch("edgetest.lib.EnvBuilder", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_notest(mock_popen, mock_builder): """Test creating a basic environment.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("setup.cfg", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_CFG)
result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=setup.cfg", "--notest"])
assert result.exit_code == 0
env_loc = Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myenv" if platform.system() == "Windows": py_loc = env_loc / "Scripts" / "python.exe" else: py_loc = env_loc / "bin" / "python"
> mock_builder.return_value.create.assert_called_with(env_loc)

def assert_called_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the last call was made with the specified arguments.
Raises an AssertionError if the args and keyword args passed in are different to the last call to the mock.""" if self.call_args is None: expected = self._format_mock_call_signature(args, kwargs) actual = 'not called.' error_message = ('expected call not found.\nExpected: %s\nActual: %s' % (expected, actual)) raise AssertionError(error_message)
def _error_message(): msg = self._format_mock_failure_message(args, kwargs) return msg expected = self._call_matcher(_Call((args, kwargs), two=True)) actual = self._call_matcher(self.call_args) if actual != expected: cause = expected if isinstance(expected, Exception) else None > raise AssertionError(_error_message()) from cause E AssertionError: expected call not found. E Expected: create(PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmpp77tkptk/.edgetest/myenv')) E Actual: create(env_dir=PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmpp77tkptk/.edgetest/myenv'))

mock_popen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4572300048'> mock_cpopen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4571709136'> mock_builder = <MagicMock name='EnvBuilder' spec='EnvBuilder' id='4574961680'>
@patch("edgetest.lib.EnvBuilder", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.core.Popen", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_reqs(mock_popen, mock_cpopen, mock_builder): """Test running tests based on the requirements file.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0) mock_cpopen.return_value.communicate.return_value = ("output", "error") type(mock_cpopen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("requirements.txt", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(REQS)
result = runner.invoke(cli)
if platform.system() == "Windows": py_myupgrade_loc = ( Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myupgrade" / "Scripts" / "python.exe" ) py_allreq_loc = ( Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "all-requirements" / "Scripts" / "python.exe" ) else: py_myupgrade_loc = Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myupgrade" / "bin" / "python" py_allreq_loc = Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "all-requirements" / "bin" / "python"
assert result.exit_code == 0
> assert mock_builder.return_value.create.call_args_list == [ call(env_dir=Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myupgrade"), call(env_dir=Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "all-requirements"), ] E AssertionError: assert [call(env_dir...quirements'))] == [call(env_dir...quirements'))] E E At index 0 diff: call(env_dir=PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmpp35w3k2d/.edgetest/myupgrade')) != call(env_dir=PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmpp35w3k2d/.edgetest/myupgrade')) E E Full diff: E - [ E - call(env_dir=PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmpp35w3k2d/.edgetest/myupgrade')), E ? ^^^^... mock_popen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4572528464'> mock_cpopen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4572188240'>
@patch("edgetest.core.Popen", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_nosetup_lower(mock_popen, mock_cpopen): """Test creating a basic environment.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0) mock_cpopen.return_value.communicate.return_value = ("output", "error") type(mock_cpopen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("pyproject.toml", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_TOML_LOWER)
result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=pyproject.toml", "--nosetup"])
assert result.exit_code == 0
env_loc = str(Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myenv_lower") if platform.system() == "Windows": py_loc = Path(env_loc) / "Scripts" / "python.exe" else: py_loc = Path(env_loc) / "bin" / "python"
> assert mock_cpopen.call_args_list == [ call( (f"{py_loc}", "-m", "pytest", "tests", "-m", "not integration"), universal_newlines=True, ) ] E AssertionError: assert [call(('/priv...ewlines=True)] == [call(('/var/...ewlines=True)] E E At index 0 diff: call(('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp28z9cbc_/.edgetest/myenv_lower/bin/python', '-m', 'pytest', 'tests', '-m', 'not integration'), universal_newlines=True) != call(('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp28z9cbc_/.edgetest/myenv_lower/bin/python', '-m', 'pytest', 'tests', '-m', 'not integration'), universal_newlines=True) E E Full diff: E - [ E - call(('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp28z9cbc_/.edgetest/myenv_lower/bin/python', '-m', 'pytest', 'tests', '-m', 'not integration'), universal_newlines... E E ...Full output truncated (5 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show

testcase.name: test_cli_notest

failure.message: AssertionError: expected call not found. Expected: create(PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp_dbmfdud/.edgetest/myenv')) Actual: create(env_dir=PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp_dbmfdud/.edgetest/myenv'))

testcase.failure:
mock_popen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4572639120'> mock_builder = <MagicMock name='EnvBuilder' spec='EnvBuilder' id='4572515536'>
@patch("edgetest.lib.EnvBuilder", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_notest(mock_popen, mock_builder): """Test creating a basic environment.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("pyproject.toml", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_TOML)
result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=pyproject.toml", "--notest"])
assert result.exit_code == 0
env_loc = Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myenv" if platform.system() == "Windows": py_loc = env_loc / "Scripts" / "python.exe" else: py_loc = env_loc / "bin" / "python"
> mock_builder.return_value.create.assert_called_with(env_loc)
/Users/nelsonmbigili/Desktop/Spring 2026/Software Analysis/migratelib-artifact/repos/capitalone@edgetest__dfc8b73c__tabulate__prettytable/tests/test_interface_toml.py:529: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='EnvBuilder().create' spec='function' id='4574983824'> args = (PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp_dbmfdud/.edgetest/myenv'),) kwargs = {} expected = call('', (PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp_dbmfdud/.edgetest/myenv'),), {}) actual = call('', (PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp_dbmfdud/.edgetest/myenv'),), {}) _error_message = <function NonCallableMock.assert_called_with.<locals>._error_message at 0x1108b4fe0> cause = None
def assert_called_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the last call was made with the specified arguments.
Raises an AssertionError if the args and keyword args passed in are different to the last call to the mock.""" if self.call_args is None: expected = self._format_mock_call_signature(args, kwargs) actual = 'not called.' error_message = ('expected call not found.\nExpected: %s\nActual: %s' % (expected, actual)) raise AssertionError(error_message)
def _error_message(): msg = self._format_mock_failure_message(args, kwargs) return msg expected = self._call_matcher(_Call((args, kwargs), two=True)) actual = self._call_matcher(self.call_args) if actual != expected: cause = expected if isinstance(expected, Exception) else None > raise AssertionError(_error_message()) from cause E AssertionError: expected call not found. E Expected: create(PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp_dbmfdud/.edgetest/myenv')) E Actual: create(env_dir=PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp_dbmfdud/.edgetest/myenv'))

testcase.name: test_cli_notest_lower

failure.message: AssertionError: expected call not found. Expected: create(PosixPath('/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp0ka3hk0z/.edgetest/myenv_lower')) Actual: create(env_dir=PosixPath('/private/var/folders/01/v7wkp0nx73l4tyy9v0_96rgh0000gn/T/tmp0ka3hk0z/.edgetest/myenv_lower'))

mock_popen = <MagicMock name='Popen' spec='Popen' id='4571704528'> mock_builder = <MagicMock name='EnvBuilder' spec='EnvBuilder' id='4571375696'>
@patch("edgetest.lib.EnvBuilder", autospec=True) @patch("edgetest.utils.Popen", autospec=True) def test_cli_notest_lower(mock_popen, mock_builder): """Test creating a basic environment.""" mock_popen.return_value.communicate.return_value = (PIP_LIST, "error") type(mock_popen.return_value).returncode = PropertyMock(return_value=0)
runner = CliRunner()
with runner.isolated_filesystem() as loc: with open("pyproject.toml", "w") as outfile: outfile.write(SETUP_TOML_LOWER)
result = runner.invoke(cli, ["--config=pyproject.toml", "--notest"])
assert result.exit_code == 0
env_loc = Path(loc) / ".edgetest" / "myenv_lower" if platform.system() == "Windows": py_loc = env_loc / "Scripts" / "python.exe" else: py_loc = env_loc / "bin" / "python"
> mock_builder.return_value.create.assert_called_with(env_loc)

def assert_called_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the last call was made with the specified arguments.
Raises an AssertionError if the args and keyword args passed in are different to the last call to the mock.""" if self.call_args is None: expected = self._format_mock_call_signature(args, kwargs) actual = 'not called.' error_message = ('expected call not found.\nExpected: %s\nActual: %s' % (expected, actual)) raise AssertionError(error_message)
def _error_message(): msg = self._format_mock_failure_message(args, kwargs) return msg expected = self._call_matcher(_Call((args, kwargs), two=True)) actual = self._call_matcher(self.call_args) if actual != expected: cause = expected if isinstance(expected, Exception) else None > raise AssertionError(_error_message()) from cause E AssertionError: expected call not found.
